rows.notes: Top-centre page title. Handwriting clear; interpreted as 'Arrearagia' exactly as written on page (old spelling). No amounts present.
Clear handwriting for description and amount. Pounds column aligned under the first ruling; reads '20', shillings and pence are '00'.
Description legible as 'Guineas pro Anno'. Amount columns clearly read 50 | 00 | 00.
Place name and date legible as 'Longwittenham ad Mie. 1738'. Amount columns read 24 | 10 | 00; slight ink variation on the '10' but column alignment is unambiguous.

Description read as 'South Newington Reet pro Anno' (Reet = rent). Pounds and shillings columns read 58 | 10. Pence appears effectively zero; there is a very faint mark possibly '1' but alignment with ruling lines suggests '00' pence—assigned 00 with moderate confidence.

rows.description: Pudlington Reet. pro Bienn: cum dimidio

Short description 'Yardington Reet pro Bienn' (appears similar name repeated). Amount clearly 20 | 00 | 00.
Reads 'Merton Reet. pro quinquennio'. Amount aligned as 26 | 10 | 00; characters clear.
Description read as 'Chase Hill pro septenrio'. Amount columns show 42 | 03 | 02; alignment and digits are clear.
Clearly 'Thrupp pro Octendecim Annis'. Amount 90 | 00 | 00 is crisp and unambiguous.

Reads 'Barrett alias Warner pro 14 Ann:'. Amount 07 | 03 | 00 is well aligned and legible.

rows.description: Big alias Lord now Youd ad Febr. Mie.

Moderate ambiguity in the middle column digit.
Reads 'Callowbarg Lane pro Anno' (spelling approximated to match manuscript). Amount clearly 02 | 17 | 09; digits are distinct and in valid ranges.
Description reads 'Bidlington Mills pro Anno'. Amount 05 | 16 | 11 is legible; pence '11' is clearly two strokes.
Reads 'Mountacute pro 20 Annis'. Amount 13 | 00 | 00 is clear.
Description 'Webb pro sequinanno' (spelling per manuscript). Amount reads 00 | 12 | 00; '12' shillings is clear and valid.

Reads 'Mynn pro triennio'. Amount columns read 04 | 13 | 04; digits are clear and within valid ranges.

Clear text 'Franklin pro Anno'. Amount 01 | 00 | 00 is crisp.
Reads 'Banister (now Franklin)'. Amount 00 | 15 | 00 clearly legible.
Description 'Bampton pro Biennio' clear. Amount 02 | 00 | 00 unambiguous.

Reads 'Marshal capons pro Biennio'. Amount appears as 00 | 05 | 10; the pence figure '10' is slightly cramped and could be '00' with stray marks, but the alignment of the two digits suggests '10' pence. Moderate confidence.
Clear 'Sadler pro Anno'. Amount 04 | 10 | 00 is distinct and within valid ranges.
Reads 'Molineux pro Biennio'. Amount 09 | 09 | 05 is clearly written; digits are legible.

Two names appear on this and the next indented line: 'Tintinhull / Napper'. The rightmost pence has a small loop after the '9' consistent with a halfpenny mark ('ob'). Interpreted as 04 | 01 | 09 1/2. Handwriting of fraction is faint -> moderate confidence.
Indented name 'Hopkins' (appears as a sub-entry). Pence has a faint trailing loop similar to a halfpenny symbol; interpreted as 00 | 12 | 07 1/2. Fraction detection is uncertain due to faint ink.
Reads 'Hardhall pro octennio'. Amount 13 | 06 | 08 is clear and well aligned.
Description read as 'Clifton ferry pro Anno'. Amount columns show 04 | 10 | 04; digits are clear.
Reads 'Bennington pro triennio'. Amount 11 | 02 | 05 legible and within valid ranges.
Description 'South Newington Tenement pro Anno' legible. Amount 01 | 07 | 10 is clear.

Description 'Medenham pro Biennio' clear. Amount 13 | 06 | 08 legible and within valid ranges.
